name: legacy-ip
description: Legacy IP MAS update: Read RTL → Reverse-engineer → Confirm changes → Delta §1-§2 → §3 → §4-§6 → §7-§9

tasks.content: [MAS] Read existing RTL and identify current interface
tasks.detail:
Read <module>.sv (existing RTL) and any existing spec/doc files (*_spec.md, *_mas.md, README).
Todo:
- [ ] Extract module name and all port declarations: Name / Width / Direction / Clock domain
- [ ] Extract all parameters with current defaults
- [ ] Note top-level instantiation context (who uses this module)
- [ ] List any existing MAS or spec document and its version
Gate: Current port table documented before reverse-engineering.

tasks.content: [MAS] Reverse-engineer current design: FSM, datapath, registers
tasks.detail:
Analyze existing RTL to reconstruct current behavior.
Todo:
- [ ] Extract FSM: states and transitions from always_ff/case blocks
- [ ] Extract register map: CSR decode logic → Offset / Name / Width / Access / Reset
- [ ] Extract datapath: operations from always_comb blocks
- [ ] Flag any undocumented behaviors, dead code, or lint issues found
- [ ] Note any discrepancies between existing spec (if any) and actual RTL
Gate: Current behavior fully documented — this is the delta baseline.

Present current design summary to the user and confirm what changes are needed.
Todo:
- [ ] Show current port table — confirm which ports to add/modify (removal = backward compat break, must be explicit)
- [ ] Show current FSM — confirm which states/transitions to add or change
- [ ] Show current register map — confirm new registers, modified bitfields, new offsets
- [ ] Confirm new interrupt sources (if any)
- [ ] Confirm timing or memory changes (if any)
- [ ] Get explicit user sign-off before writing any MAS delta
Gate: User has approved the change list.

Update §1 and §2 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md (create if missing).
Todo:
- [ ] §1: Update description if feature set changed; add 'Changed in vX.Y' note
- [ ] §2 Ports: Add new ports at end of port table; mark changed ports with 'Changed in vX.Y'; do NOT reorder existing ports
- [ ] §2 Parameters: Add new parameters; preserve existing parameter names and defaults
- [ ] Unchanged sections: copy verbatim from existing MAS — do not rewrite
Gate: Backward compatibility preserved for all existing ports.

Update §3 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] Add new feature sections for new functionality
- [ ] Update FSM state table: add new states/transitions; mark changed rows with 'Changed in vX.Y'
- [ ] Verify all existing FSM transitions are preserved (no regression)
- [ ] Unchanged features: copy verbatim
Gate: No existing FSM state removed without explicit user approval.

Update §4, §5, §6 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] §4: Add new registers at new offsets (do NOT reuse existing offsets); update bitfield detail for changed registers; mark with 'Changed in vX.Y'
- [ ] §5: Add new interrupt sources with new bit positions; preserve existing bit assignments
- [ ] §6: Add new memory instances if needed; update latency/depth if changed
- [ ] If a section is unchanged: copy verbatim
Gate: No existing register offset or interrupt bit reassigned.

tasks.content: [MAS] §7-§9 delta: Timing + RTL Notes + DV Plan — MAS complete
tasks.detail:
Update §7, §8, §9 in <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
Todo:
- [ ] §7: Update clock/latency/throughput only if timing changed; add CDC notes for new crossings
- [ ] §8: Add lint waivers or coding notes for new logic; preserve existing rules
- [ ] §9 DV Plan: Add new test sequences (S_NEW_1..N) for new features — do NOT remove existing sequences; update coverage goals for new FSM states and registers; add SVA for new assertions
- [ ] All 9 sections complete and internally consistent
Gate: All 9 sections filled. Save final delta MAS as <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md.
